intent: Build a self-custodial mobile-first Lightning wallet using the Breez SDK, with send/receive over BOLT11 invoices, LSP-assisted channels, and a clean Bitcoin-design UI.

selectedStack.name: breez-sdk
selectedStack.reason: Provides an all-in-one self-custodial Lightning stack (node, LSP, on-chain swaps) behind a single SDK surface.

selectedStack.name: lightning-network
selectedStack.reason: Underlying payment layer for instant, low-fee transfers.

selectedStack.name: bolt11-invoices
selectedStack.reason: Standard invoice format for request/pay flows in the wallet UI.

selectedStack.name: bitcoin-design
selectedStack.reason: Bitcoin Design Guide patterns for wallet UX (backups, fees, receive/send).

selectedStack.name: loupe
selectedStack.reason: Static analysis pass planned for the generated wallet code before release.

architecture.summary: A mobile-first React frontend talks to the Breez SDK through a thin adapter. Lightning send/receive runs over LSP-assisted channels; key material stays on device.
architecture.frontend: React + TypeScript wallet UI (send, receive, history, backup)
architecture.backend: Breez SDK embedded node (no custodial server); optional LSP for inbound liquidity
architecture.bitcoinLayer: Bitcoin L1 for on-chain swaps and channel funding
architecture.securityLayer: On-device key storage, encrypted backups, planned Loupe static scan
assumptions: An LSP is available for inbound liquidity during onboarding.
Network connectivity is intermittent; flows must tolerate retries.
risks: LSP trust for inbound liquidity and routing.
Backup/restore UX is critical — lost seed means lost funds.
Fee spikes on L1 can affect swap and channel operations.

securityNotes.note: Seed and channel keys are held on device.
securityNotes.recommendation: Use platform secure storage (Keychain/Keystore); never log or transmit key material.

securityNotes.note: Loss of seed or channel state can mean loss of funds.
securityNotes.recommendation: Enforce verified backup before enabling receive; document recovery flow.

securityNotes.note: Inbound liquidity relies on a Lightning Service Provider.
securityNotes.recommendation: Disclose LSP dependence; allow user-configurable LSP where possible.

securityNotes.note: Malformed or expired BOLT11 invoices can confuse the pay flow.
securityNotes.recommendation: Validate invoices client-side and surface clear expiry/amount errors.

generatedApp.summary: Scaffold for a self-custodial Lightning wallet: Breez SDK adapter, send/receive screens, backup flow, and a planned Loupe security pass. File metadata only — no source bodies in this demo.

files.purpose: Thin adapter over the Breez SDK

instructions: Install dependencies and provide a Breez API key via environment (never commit it).
Initialize the Breez SDK adapter on app start.
Complete the backup flow before enabling receive.
Run the planned Loupe static analysis pass before any release build.
warnings: This is scaffold/demo metadata only — not production-audited code.
Breez API credentials must be supplied at runtime and kept out of the client bundle/repo.
trustAssumptions: Self-custodial: the user controls keys; there is no custodial server.
Inbound liquidity depends on an LSP.
securityNotes: Key material stays on device in secure storage.
Backups are encrypted; recovery is documented.

securityTools.name: Loupe
notes: Static analysis pass is PLANNED for the generated wallet code.
Not executed in this demo build — status is 'planned' only.
